summary: Round 4 Synthesizer accepted 15 of 18 (83%) — heavy emphasis on the starved exit pipeline (X127-X133) plus 4 entries filling cross-channel and contrarian-sentiment gaps. Three revisions correct semantic defects: a static-state exit firing wrong-sided, an unsigned-funding new-listing entry, and a universal risk gate that would null-compose with R6 by construction. Zero rejections — Devil's possibly_fail flags are monitoring concerns, not logical defects, and let paper-shadow be the second filter per Round 3+ inclusive posture.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Exit when funding flips against position AND OI starts decaying — orthogonal microstructure trigger before price reverses.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Devil flagged funding 8h cadence (slow flip rate) — accept anyway as paper-shadow signal; Quant ample (~2400), Risk acceptable. First exit kernel pairing funding sign-flip with OI decay.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Exit position when smart-money top-trader position ratio enters decline regime — leading indicator of impending reversal.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: 5min snapshot lag acceptable for >10min holds; Risk requires verification _dec resolves via backward-rolling slope only — flag in monitor not blocker.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Lock breakeven when premium_bps collapses to neutral band — premium decay signals exhaustion of the futures-spot spread driver.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Devil+Quant flag possible degeneracy (modal range for liquid perps); accept-with-watch — if >30% of fires are <30s post-entry, retune band to ±2 bps in R5.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Quick exit when basis_rate normalizes to ±1% band — basis convergence often precedes mean reversion in perp markets.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Scale ambiguity (decimal vs bps) must be resolved at data-loader before scoring; accept for paper-shadow with first-100-fires distribution dump as Devil suggested.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Tighten trailing stop when volume drops below p25 — momentum exhaustion signal that classical trail kernels ignore.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Cleanest of the exit batch — Devil seems_ok, Quant ample (~1250), Risk acceptable pending p25 backward-rolling verification.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Close long when 5m taker buy ratio inverts below 0.45 — aggressive sellers taking control of order flow.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Devil flags risk of early-exit on profitable pullbacks; pair with breakeven kernels (not trail) — paper-shadow must compare vs counterfactual fixed-TP hold.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Widen TP and tighten SL during US session — different volatility regime than Asian; static exits underperform across sessions.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: session=US already binds the upper hour bound (canonically ≤21 UTC); engine-side TP/SL multipliers must actually deviate from baseline for this kernel to differentiate.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Long when OI surges with deeply negative funding AND discount premium — classic short-squeeze setup with three orthogonal confirmations.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Quant estimates ~18 triggers/30d (too_few) — accept-with-watch; Risk caution on live BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump-long overlap, paper-shadow must enforce one-position-per-symbol cap across research+live pools.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Short pump events when top-trader ratio is already crowded long — fade smart-money exhaustion with volume confirmation.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Cleanest entry of the batch — Devil seems_ok, Quant tight (~62 triggers, adequate), Risk acceptable; 5min top_trader staleness is a known cross-batch caveat.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Short when basis blows out >5% AND premium extreme AND OI surging — classic perp overheating, mean reversion setup.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Sample ~8 (too_few) and basis_rate scale ambiguity (5% vs 5bps) flagged; accept for option value — high-conviction overheating signature, retune threshold after first run if zero triggers.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Long Reserved6 crashes when global retail is extremely short AND volume is top-decile — squeeze fuel meets capitulation.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Sample ~6 (too_few) but R6/long is empirically alive (E316-E327 lift +0.343); accept for high-EV thin sample, enforce 1-position-per-symbol cap due to concentration risk.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Gate trades to mid-cap symbols during US session with healthy volume — best signal-to-noise band per session-cap analysis.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Clean accept — first joint session × marketcap × volume filter in 121 filters; Devil seems_ok, Quant ample (~735), Risk acceptable pending quote_volume_24h trailing-window verification.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Only allow trades on low-liquidity symbols with funding extreme but excluding ultra-new listings — wild-but-not-untested universe.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Slippage divergence is the main concern (Devil+Risk both flag); MANDATE realistic-fill paper-shadow with ≥25% edge haircut before any combo using F123 is promoted to live.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Trigger long only when OI_Price pump AND pricechange pump fire within 30s of each other AND taker flow confirms — synchronous multi-channel agreement.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: 30s sync constraint is descriptive, not parser-enforced — effective archetype is single-channel oi+pump+taker; Risk flags HIGH live conflict with BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump-long bot, paper-shadow must model combined-exposure cap.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Short Reserved6 events when OI_Price already flagged within 60s AND symbol is low-liquidity AND retail is leaning long — liquidation cascade setup.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Sample ~16 (too_few), tautology (OI→R6 may be same event) + low-liq slippage concerns; accept-with-watch — verify OI→R6 gap >5s in historical alerts, mandate ≥30% slippage haircut for shorts.

revised_archetypes.revision_reason: Devil+Quant both flag wrong-sided semantics: original novel_dim 'global_long_ratio_high' encodes a static state, which means 'exit short while crowd is long' — that is the OPPOSITE of taking profit on squeeze relief (crowd-long is the squeeze fuel you want to hold the short for). Without a transition operator in the DSL, reframed to use global_short_ratio_high as the 'crowd has flipped' positive-exit signal.
revised_archetypes.revised_notes: Close short when global short ratio rises to high — crowd has flipped from long-extreme to short-leaning, indicating the squeeze played out and profit-taking is appropriate. Channel=*, side=short. Sentiment-flip exit, orthogonal to all 5 price/time exit kernels.

revised_archetypes.revision_reason: Devil flags that funding_abs_extreme is unsigned and could fire on highly POSITIVE funding (bearish for long) — original conditions are direction-blind for a directional long entry. Added explicit funding<=0 gate to enforce sign-awareness; conjunction now requires funding to be both extreme magnitude AND on the favorable side for longs.
revised_archetypes.revised_notes: Long new listings (≤30d) when funding is negative AND at extreme magnitude AND liquidity is mid — early-stage volatility with absorbable size; sign-aware funding gate ensures the extreme is on the favorable side for longs. Channel=pricechange, side=long.

revised_archetypes.revision_reason: Devil+Quant+Risk all flag that move_pct<=3 EXCLUDES Reserved6 (defined as 8-15%/180s extreme moves) by construction; if synthesizer composes this risk archetype with any R6 entry the result is a structurally null combo (zero triggers). day_change_pct cap of ±10 also kills most pricechange-channel pump events on small caps. Thresholds relaxed so that R6 boundary moves and pricechange small-cap pumps can still compose with this gate while still capping out-of-bounds chases.
revised_archetypes.revised_notes: Skip entries when symbol already moved >8% on the current bar OR >20% on the day — chase-prevention to protect against late-stage continuation traps; thresholds relaxed from R3 spec so R6 (8-15%/180s) and pricechange small-cap pumps can still compose. Channel=*, side=NA.

next_round_focus: Round 5 should pivot from individual archetype generation to entry × exit COMPOSITION — most R4 accepted units (X127-X133, E330-E333, F122-F123, C063-C064) are individually sound but unproven jointly. Propose 4-6 best-of-batch entry × exit candidate combos (e.g. E331 contrarian-pump-short × X132 taker-inversion-exit; E333 R6/long × X127 funding-flip-OI-decay) plus 2-3 transition-aware exit kernels now that x_global_long_normalize_short proved static-state exits can be silently wrong-sided.
